zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Waterman於大約1494年出生在英國英格蘭哈普爾安多福時，她的父親William Waterman26歲，母親Unknown26歲。她於大約1525年與James Tutt結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1556年7月3日在英國英格蘭哈普爾爾波爾頓享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: Waterman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Dutch: occupational name for a boatman or ferryman, or a water carrier (the English name was perhaps sometimes synonymous with Middle English waterlader or waterberer, denoting a man who carried fresh water from a spring or stream for drinking or domestic use), or a topographic name for someone who lived by a pond or stretch of water (see Water 2).
English: occupational name from the Middle English personal name Water or Walter + man, meaning ‘servant of Wa(l)ter’.
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Wassermann   or Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Wasserman  .

variants.name: Water
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegfried, Wolf.
English: variant of Walter , a common medieval pronunciation of which was Water.
English and German (Rhineland): topographic name for someone who lived by a stretch of water, Middle English and Low German water.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Waterman was born about 1494, in Andover, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Waterman, was 26 and her mother, Unknown, was 26. She married James Tutt about 1525. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 3 July 1556, in Chilbolton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Waterman est née environ 1494, à Andover, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Waterman, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Unknown, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé James Tutt environ 1525. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 3 juillet 1556, à Chilbolton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Waterman um 1494 in Andover, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Waterman 26 und ihre Mutter Unknown 26. Sie hat um 1525 James Tutt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 3. Juli 1556 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Chilbolton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Waterman è nata il circa 1494 nel Andover, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Waterman, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Unknown, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato James Tutt circa 1525. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 3 luglio 1556, nel Chilbolton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Waterman(이)가 대략 1494년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 앤도버에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Waterman(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Unknown(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1525년 James Tutt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1556년 7월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 치볼턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.
